task_completion.description: Verify the document includes the required document number in the header/title and page numbers in the footer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for 'FRS-PC3-2024-012' at the top of the document (either as a title page or in the header area) and page numbers visible at the bottom of the pages in the footer area.

task_completion.description: Verify an auto-generated Table of Contents is present.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a Table of Contents section. In OpenOffice Writer, auto-generated ToCs typically have a light gray background behind the text and leader dots connecting to page numbers. You may also see the 'Insert Index/Table' dialog in the trajectory.

task_completion.description: Verify 'Heading 1' and 'Heading 2' styles are used for sections and subsections.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the formatting toolbar (top left) when the cursor is on a section title; it should display 'Heading 1' or 'Heading 2' instead of 'Default' or 'Text body'. The headings should appear visually distinct (larger, bold).

task_completion.description: Verify the document includes the 5 functional modules, non-functional requirements, and external interfaces.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for section headers or text mentioning 'Authentication', 'Scheduling', 'Medical Records', 'Messaging', 'Billing', 'Non-functional requirements', and 'External interfaces'.

task_completion.description: Verify requirements and use cases are presented in data tables with specific columns.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for grid tables inserted into the document. The tables must have columns explicitly labeled 'ID', 'Description', and 'Priority', and be populated with the requirement data.

task_completion.description: Verify the document is saved as an ODF Text Document with the correct filename.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the title bar of the OpenOffice Writer window. It should display 'FRS-PC3-2024-012.odt', indicating the file was saved correctly.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the OpenOffice Writer GUI to create and format the document.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for screenshots showing the agent clicking through OpenOffice menus (e.g., Insert -> Indexes and Tables, Insert -> Header/Footer) rather than using terminal commands or Python scripts to generate the .odt file.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the actual data from the provided JSON file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the agent opening or viewing 'patientconnect_requirements.json' and transferring its specific healthcare data (e.g., FHIR, Epic EHR, Twilio) into the document, rather than typing generic placeholder text.

integrity.description: Verify the Table of Contents was auto-generated and not manually typed.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the gray field shading behind the Table of Contents text, which indicates an auto-generated field in OpenOffice, or evidence of the agent using the 'Insert Index/Table' feature rather than manually typing out headings and page numbers.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires creating an FRS document in Apache OpenOffice Writer. The output file must be named 'FRS-PC3-2024-012.odt'. The document must contain 5 functional modules (Authentication, Scheduling, Medical Records, Messaging, Billing), Non-Functional Requirements, and External Interfaces. It must use Heading 1 and Heading 2 styles, include an auto-generated Table of Contents, have page numbers in the footer, and feature the document number 'FRS-PC3-2024-012' in a title page or header. Requirements must be formatted in tables with 'ID', 'Description', and 'Priority' columns. Note: The source JSON does not contain the word 'HIPAA', so do not penalize if it is missing, but 'PatientConnect', 'FHIR', and 'Pinnacle' should be present in the text.
